Any test files for FakeGitRemoteOps - search for files containing "FakeGitRemoteOps" 3. The `tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline/` directory - list all files in it
FIRST check docs/learned/index.md for existing documentation on this topic. Read relevant docs before exploring raw files. Then explore codebase as necessary.

1→<!-- AUTO-GENERATED FILE - DO NOT EDIT DIRECTLY --> 2→<!-- Edit source frontmatter, then run 'erk docs sync' to regenerate. --> 3→ 4→# Agent Documentation 5→ 6→Before starting work, scan the read-when conditions below. 7→If your current task matches, read the linked document **before writing code**. 8→ 9→## Categories 10→ 11→- [architecture/](architecture/) — Explore when working on core patterns (dry-run, gateways, subprocess, shell integration). Add docs here for cross-cutting technical patterns. 12→- [capabilities/](capabilities/) 13→- [changelog/](changelog/) 14→- [checklists/](checklists/) 15→- [ci/](ci/) 16→- [claude-code/](claude-code/) 17→- [cli/](cli/) — Explore when building CLI commands or output formatting. Add docs here for Click patterns and terminal UX. 18→- [commands/](commands/) — Explore when creating or optimizing slash commands. Add docs here for command authoring patterns. 19→- [config/](config/) 20→- [configuration/](configuration/) 21→- [documentation/](documentation/) — Explore when structuring or writing documentation. Add docs here for documentation methodology and best practices. 22→- [erk/](erk/) — Explore when working with erk-specific workflows (worktrees, PR sync, Graphite). Add docs here for erk user-facing features. 23→- [erk-dev/](erk-dev/) 24→- [gateway/](gateway/) 25→- [hooks/](hooks/) — Explore when creating or debugging hooks. Add docs here for hook development patterns. 26→- [integrations/](integrations/) 27→- [objectives/](objectives/) 28→- [planning/](planning/) — Explore when working with plans, .erk/impl-context/ folders, or agent delegation. session.tool_result:
1→"""Unit tests for RealGit with mocked subprocess calls. 2→ 3→These tests verify that RealGit correctly constructs subprocess commands 4→for external tools (git) without actually executing them. 5→""" 6→ 7→from pathlib import Path 8→from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch 9→ 10→from erk_shared.gateway.git.real import RealGit 11→ 12→ 13→def test_list_remote_branches() -> None: 14→ """Test git branch -r with mocked subprocess.""" 15→ with patch("subprocess.run") as mock_run: 16→ # Arrange: Configure mock to return sample remote branches 17→ mock_run.return_value = MagicMock( 18→ stdout="origin/main
origin/feature-1
origin/feature-2
", 19→ returncode=0, 20→ ) 21→ 22→ # Act: Call the method 23→ ops = RealGit() 24→ branches = ops.branch.list_remote_branches(Path("/test/repo")) 25→ 26→ # Assert: Verify command construction 27→ mock_run.assert_called_once() 28→ call_args = mock_run.call_args 29→ assert call_args[0][0] == ["git", "branch", "-r", "--format=%(refname:short)"] 30→ assert call_args[1]["cwd"] == Path("/test/repo")
... [110 lines omitted] ...

1→"""Tests for FakeGitRemoteOps.""" 2→ 3→from pathlib import Path 4→ 5→from erk_shared.gateway.git.fake import PushedBranch 6→from erk_shared.gateway.git.remote_ops.fake import FakeGitRemoteOps 7→from erk_shared.gateway.git.remote_ops.types import ( 8→ PullRebaseError, 9→ PullRebaseResult, 10→ PushError, 11→ PushResult, 12→) 13→ 14→ 15→class TestPushToRemote: 16→ def test_returns_push_result_on_success(self) -> None: 17→ ops = FakeGitRemoteOps() 18→ result = ops.push_to_remote( 19→ Path("/repo"), "origin", "main", set_upstream=False, force=False 20→ ) 21→ assert isinstance(result, PushResult) 22→ 23→ def test_returns_push_error_when_configured(self) -> None: 24→ error = PushError(message="rejected") 25→ ops = FakeGitRemoteOps(push_to_remote_error=error) 26→ result = ops.push_to_remote( 27→ Path("/repo"), "origin", "main", set_upstream=False, force=False 28→ ) 29→ assert isinstance(result, PushError) 30→ assert result.message == "rejected"
... [44 lines omitted] ...
39→ def test_does_not_track_pushed_branch_on_error(self) -> None: 40→ ops = FakeGitRemoteOps(push_to_remote_error=PushError(message="rejected")) 51→ def test_returns_pull_rebase_error_when_configured(self) -> None: 52→ error = PullRebaseError(message="conflict") 53→ ops = FakeGitRemoteOps(pull_rebase_error=error) 55→ assert isinstance(result, PullRebaseError) 64→ def test_tracks_pull_rebase_call_even_on_error(self) -> None: 66→ ops = FakeGitRemoteOps(pull_rebase_error=PullRebaseError(message="conflict"))

1→--- 2→title: Gateway Fake Testing Exemplar 3→read_when: 4→ - "writing tests for gateway fakes that return discriminated unions" 5→ - "deciding whether mutation tracking should occur on error paths" 6→ - "implementing a new fake gateway method with error injection" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "creating a fake gateway without constructor-injected error configuration" 9→ warning: "Fakes must accept error variants at construction time (e.g., push_to_remote_error=PushError(...)) to enable failure injection in tests." 10→ - action: "using truthiness checks or .success on discriminated union results in tests" 11→ warning: "Always use isinstance() for type narrowing. Never check bool(result) or result.success — these bypass the type system." 12→ - action: "tracking mutations before checking error configuration in a fake method" 13→ warning: "Decide deliberately: should this operation track even on failure? push_to_remote skips tracking on error (nothing happened), pull_rebase always tracks (the attempt matters). Match the real operation's semantics." 14→--- 15→ 16→# Gateway Fake Testing Exemplar 17→ 18→This document captures the cross-cutting design decisions in fake gateway testing — specifically, the three patterns that every fake test must exercise and the subtle tracking-on-error decision that differs between operations. 19→ 20→## Why Fakes Need Their Own Tests 21→ 22→Fakes are production code for tests. A broken fake silently produces false-positive test results across the entire test suite. The `tests/unit/fakes/` directory exists to verify that fakes honor their ABC contracts and that their error-injection and mutation-tracking machinery works correctly. 23→ 24→<!-- Source: tests/unit/fakes/test_fake_git_remote_ops.py --> 25→ 26→See `test_fake_git_remote_ops.py` in `tests/unit/fakes/` for the canonical exemplar. 27→ 28→## Three Required Test Categories Per Operation 29→ 30→Every fake method that returns a discriminated union needs three test categories:
... [66 lines omitted] ...
35→| **Error injection** | Constructor-configured error is returned | Proves failure paths work without modifying fake internals | 40→## The Tracking-on-Error Decision 42→The most subtle design choice in fake implementation is whether mutation tracking should occur when an error is configured. This is not arbitrary — it follows the real operation's semantics: 44→| Operation | Tracks on error? | Rationale | 52→Compare `FakeGitRemoteOps.push_to_remote()` (early-returns error before tracking) with `FakeGitRemoteOps.pull_rebase()` (tracks first, then checks error) in `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/remote_ops/fake.py`. 54→**When implementing a new fake method**, ask: "If the real operation fails, did a side effect still occur?" If yes, track before checking the error. If no, check the error first and skip tracking. 81→if isinstance(result, PushError): 85→**Testing only the happy path:** Every discriminated union method needs both success and error tests. A fake that only tests success could silently break error injection for all consumers. 90→- [Discriminated Union Error Handling](../architecture/discriminated-union-error-handling.md) — Design decisions for when to use unions vs exceptions
session.assistant: Excellent! Now let me get more details on the FakeGitRemoteOps implementation and read the test_real_git.py to understand the test_branch_exists_on_remote pattern better:
session.tool_result:
1→"""Fake implementation of Git remote operations for testing.""" 2→ 3→from __future__ import annotations 4→ 5→from pathlib import Path 6→from typing import TYPE_CHECKING 7→ 8→from erk_shared.gateway.git.remote_ops.abc import GitRemoteOps 9→from erk_shared.gateway.git.remote_ops.types import ( 10→ PullRebaseError, 11→ PullRebaseResult, 12→ PushError, 13→ PushResult, 14→) 15→ 16→if TYPE_CHECKING: 17→ # Import PushedBranch type from parent module to avoid circular import 18→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.fake import PushedBranch 19→ 20→ 21→class FakeGitRemoteOps(GitRemoteOps): 22→ """In-memory fake implementation of Git remote operations. 23→ 24→ This fake accepts pre-configured state in its constructor and tracks 25→ mutations for test assertions. 26→ 27→ Constructor Injection: 28→ --------------------- 29→ - remote_urls: Mapping of (repo_root, remote_name) -> remote URL 30→ - pull_branch_raises: Exception to raise when pull_branch() is called
... [182 lines omitted] ...
31→ - push_to_remote_error: PushError to return when push_to_remote() is called 32→ - pull_rebase_error: PullRebaseError to return when pull_rebase() is called 48→ fetch_branch_raises: Exception | None = None, 49→ pull_branch_raises: Exception | None = None, 50→ push_to_remote_error: PushError | None = None, 51→ pull_rebase_error: PullRebaseError | None = None, 57→ fetch_branch_raises: Exception to raise when fetch_branch() is called 58→ pull_branch_raises: Exception to raise when pull_branch() is called 59→ push_to_remote_error: PushError to return when push_to_remote() is called 60→ pull_rebase_error: PullRebaseError to return when pull_rebase() is called 66→ self._push_to_remote_error = push_to_remote_error 67→ self._pull_rebase_error = pull_rebase_error 107→ ) -> PushResult | PushError: 108→ """Record push to remote, or return error if failure configured.""" 112→ if self._push_to_remote_error is not None: 113→ return self._push_to_remote_error 121→ ) -> PullRebaseResult | PullRebaseError: 124→ Tracks call for test assertions. Returns configured error if set. 127→ if self._pull_rebase_error is not None: 128→ return self._pull_rebase_error 135→ ValueError: If remote doesn't exist or has no URL 139→ raise ValueError(f"Remote '{remote}' not found in repository")

1→"""Integration tests for git operations. 2→ 3→These tests verify that RealGit correctly handles git operations with real git repositories. 4→Integration tests use actual git subprocess calls to validate the abstractions. 5→""" 6→ 7→import subprocess 8→from pathlib import Path 9→ 10→import pytest 11→ 12→from erk_shared.gateway.git.real import RealGit 13→from tests.integration.conftest import ( 14→ GitSetup, 15→ GitWithDetached, 16→ GitWithExistingBranch, 17→ GitWithWorktrees, 18→ init_git_repo, 19→) 20→ 21→ 22→def test_list_worktrees_single_repo(git_ops: GitSetup) -> None: 23→ """Test listing worktrees returns only main repository when no worktrees exist.""" 24→ worktrees = git_ops.git.worktree.list_worktrees(git_ops.repo) 25→ 26→ assert len(worktrees) == 1 27→ assert worktrees[0].path == git_ops.repo 28→ assert worktrees[0].branch == "main" 29→ 30→
... [1322 lines omitted] ...
192→ """Test validate_trunk_branch raises RuntimeError when branch doesn't exist.""" 202→ with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match="does not exist in repository"): 432→ # This would have raised RuntimeError("Command not found...") before the fix
session.assistant: Perfect! Now let me check the testing documentation to get a comprehensive view:
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→title: Erk Test Reference 3→last_audited: "2026-02-13 00:00 PT" 4→audit_result: clean 5→read_when: 6→ - "writing tests for erk" 7→ - "using erk fakes" 8→ - "running erk test commands" 9→tripwires: 10→ - action: "modifying business logic in src/ without adding a test" 11→ warning: "Bug fixes require regression tests (fails before, passes after). Features require behavior tests." 12→ - action: "implementing interactive prompts with ctx.console.confirm()" 13→ warning: "Ensure FakeConsole in test fixture is configured with `confirm_responses` parameter. Array length must match prompt count exactly — too few causes IndexError, too many indicates a removed prompt. See tests/commands/submit/test_existing_branch_detection.py for examples." 14→ - action: "accessing FakeGit properties in tests" 15→ warning: "FakeGit has top-level properties (e.g., `git.staged_files`, `git.deleted_branches`, `git.added_worktrees`). Worktree operations delegate to an internal FakeWorktree sub-gateway." 16→ - action: "asserting on fake-specific properties in tests using `build_workspace_test_context` with `use_graphite=True`" 17→ warning: "Production wrappers (e.g., `GraphiteBranchManager`) do not expose fake tracking properties like `submitted_branches`. Assert on observable behavior (CLI output, return values) instead of accessing fake internals through the wrapper." 18→ - action: "asserting on YAML metadata field values with exact string matching" 19→ warning: 'Assert on key-only format (''field_name:''), not ''field_name: "value"''. YAML serialization differs from Python repr.' 20→ - action: "changing cleanup or deletion behavior without updating test assertions" 21→ warning: "When behavior changes from 'delete X' to 'preserve X', update test assertions to verify the new behavior (e.g., assert file persists instead of asserting it was deleted). Stale assertions silently validate the old behavior." 22→ score: 4 23→ - action: "asserting user_output() content against capsys stdout" 24→ warning: "user_output() routes to stderr. When testing code that calls user_output(), assert against capsys.readouterr().err, not .out." 25→ - action: "using context_for_test() with wrong parameter name for issues" 26→ warning: "erk-shared uses github_issues= parameter, src/erk uses issues= parameter. These are NOT interchangeable — using wrong name causes TypeError." 27→--- 28→ 29→# Erk Test Reference 30→
... [125 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant:
Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me provide a comprehensive report.
## Report: Test Files and Patterns for Git Remote Operations
I've thoroughly explored the codebase and documentation. Here's what I found:
### 1. Real Git Test File: `tests/real/test_real_git.py`
**Location:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/real/test_real_git.py`
**Test Pattern for `test_branch_exists_on_remote`:**
Lines 70-117 show the canonical test pattern:
```python def test_branch_exists_on_remote_when_exists() -> None: """Test git ls-remote returns True when branch exists on remote.""" with patch("subprocess.run") as mock_run: # Arrange: Configure mock to return branch ref (branch exists) mock_run.return_value = MagicMock( stdout="abc123def456\trefs/heads/feature-branch\n", returncode=0, )
# Act: Call the method ops = RealGit() exists = ops.branch.branch_exists_on_remote(Path("/test/repo"), "origin", "feature-branch")
# Assert: Verify command construction mock_run.assert_called_once() call_args = mock_run.call_args assert call_args[0][0] == ["git", "ls-remote", "origin", "feature-branch"] assert call_args[1]["cwd"] == Path("/test/repo") assert call_args[1]["capture_output"] is True assert call_args[1]["text"] is True assert call_args[1]["check"] is False
# Assert: Verify result assert exists is True ```
**Key Pattern Elements:** - Uses `patch("subprocess.run")` to mock subprocess calls - Three assertion sections: command construction, arguments verification, and result validation - Tests both success case (lines 70-93) and failure case (lines 96-117) - Also covers edge case with whitespace (lines 120-134)
---
### 2. FakeGitRemoteOps Test Files
**Test file for FakeGitRemoteOps:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/fakes/test_fake_git_remote_ops.py`
**Files containing "FakeGitRemoteOps":** - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/fakes/test_fake_git_remote_ops.py` - Unit tests - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/remote_ops/fake.py` - Implementation - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/remote_ops/__init__.py` - Import location - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/fake.py` - Parent composition - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/testing/gateway-fake-testing-exemplar.md` - Documentation
**FakeGitRemoteOps Test Pattern (lines 15-68):**
```python class TestPushToRemote: def test_returns_push_result_on_success(self) -> None: ops = FakeGitRemoteOps() result = ops.push_to_remote( Path("/repo"), "origin", "main", set_upstream=False, force=False ) assert isinstance(result, PushResult)
def test_returns_push_error_when_configured(self) -> None: error = PushError(message="rejected") ops = FakeGitRemoteOps(push_to_remote_error=error) result = ops.push_to_remote( Path("/repo"), "origin", "main", set_upstream=False, force=False ) assert isinstance(result, PushError) assert result.message == "rejected"
def test_tracks_pushed_branch_on_success(self) -> None: ops = FakeGitRemoteOps() ops.push_to_remote(Path("/repo"), "origin", "feature", set_upstream=True, force=False) assert ops.pushed_branches == [ PushedBranch(remote="origin", branch="feature", set_upstream=True, force=False) ]
def test_does_not_track_pushed_branch_on_error(self) -> None: ops = FakeGitRemoteOps(push_to_remote_error=PushError(message="rejected")) ops.push_to_remote(Path("/repo"), "origin", "main", set_upstream=False, force=False) assert ops.pushed_branches == [] ```
**Three Required Test Categories Per Operation (from documentation):** 1. **Default success** - No-arg construction returns success variant 2. **Error injection** - Constructor-configured error is returned 3. **Mutation tracking** - Operations record calls via read-only properties
**Key Design Decision:** `push_to_remote` doesn't track on error (early return), but `pull_rebase` tracks even on error (lines 58-68 in test file).
---
### 3. Submit Pipeline Test Directory
**Location:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline/`
**Files (14 total):** 1. `__init__.py` - Package marker 2. `test_capture_existing_pr_body.py` - PR body capture 3. `test_cleanup_impl_for_submit.py` - Implementation cleanup 4. `test_commit_wip.py` - Work-in-progress commit 5. `test_core_submit_flow.py` - Core submission flow 6. `test_enhance_with_graphite.py` - Graphite enhancement 7. `test_extract_diff.py` - Diff extraction 8. `test_fetch_plan_context.py` - Plan context fetching 9. `test_finalize_pr.py` - PR finalization 10. `test_generate_description.py` - Description generation 11. `test_graphite_first_flow.py` - Graphite-first workflow 12. `test_link_pr_to_objective_nodes.py` - Objective node linking 13. `test_prepare_state.py` - State preparation 14. `test_run_pipeline.py` - Pipeline execution
---
### 4. Documentation References
**Key Documentation:** - **Gateway Fake Testing Exemplar:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/testing/gateway-fake-testing-exemplar.md` - Explains the 3 test categories for every fake gateway method - Details the "tracking-on-error" decision pattern - Covers mutation tracking via `link_mutation_tracking()`
- **Erk Test Reference:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/testing/testing.md` - Complete test structure and patterns - FakeGit reference documentation - Test running commands and directory structure
---
### 5. Integration Test File
**Location:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/integration/test_real_git.py`
Contains comprehensive integration tests with actual git operations (1347 lines total). Notable sections: - Worktree operations (real git worktree add/remove/list) - Branch operations (create, checkout, detect trunk) - Commit operations (create, amend, message retrieval) - Rebase operations (conflicts, resolution, continuation) - Remote operations (pull_rebase, push) - lines 988-1039 show `pull_rebase` integration test
---
### Key Testing Architecture
``` tests/ ├── unit/fakes/ # Layer 1: Tests OF fake implementations │ └── test_fake_git_remote_ops.py ├── real/ # Layer 2: Mocked subprocess tests │ └── test_real_git.py # Tests command construction via mocks ├── integration/ # Layer 2b: Real I/O tests │ └── test_real_git.py # Tests with actual git subprocess calls └── unit/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline/ # Layer 3: CLI tests using fakes ├── test_core_submit_flow.py ├── test_graphite_first_flow.py └── ... (14 files total) ```
All findings are absolute paths as requested, and the codebase demonstrates clear separation between fake testing, real implementation testing with mocks, and integration testing with actual subprocess calls.
